message:
🌱 opentofu: Allow evaluation of constant locals and variables prior to module configuration (#1876)

* 🌱 Add opentofu: Allow evaluation of constant locals and variables prior to mission

* 🌱 Add opentofu: Allow evaluation of constant locals and variables prior to mission

Signed-off-by: github-actions[bot] <41898282+github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: replace closed opentofu PR #1107 with merged PR #1649

The original fix PR opentofu/opentofu#1107 was closed without merging.
Replace with opentofu/opentofu#1649 which was the merged RFC for the
constant locals and variables feature.

Signed-off-by: Andrew Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

---------

Signed-off-by: github-actions[bot] <41898282+github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>
Signed-off-by: Andrew Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>
Co-authored-by: github-actions[bot] <41898282+github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>

+{

+    "description": "Allow evaluation of constant locals and variables prior to module configuration. Requested by 150+ users.",
+    "type": "feature",
+    "status": "completed",
+    "steps": [
+      {
+        "title": "Check current opentofu setup",
+        "description": "Verify your opentofu version and configuration:\n```bash\ntofu version\n```\nThis feature requires a working opentofu installation."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Review opentofu configuration",
+        "description": "Review the relevant opentofu configuration:\nMany users expect to be able to DRY up their large configurations by using locals/variables in module sources and backend configurations (among other places)."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Apply the fix for Allow evaluation of constant locals and variables prior to…",
+        "description": "Yes that would be the largest functional difference on the CLI side.  Defaults could still be used if a variable is no passed in via -var or tfvars.  If a default is not given and it is not specified it would be considered missing and result in an error.\n\nWe would handle it the same way we handle someone editing the TF files between init and plan.  We would provide them with an error about what changed between init/plan.  If that is insufficient, we could track the values provided as vars to\n\nSee the source issue for community-verified solutions."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Verify the feature works",
+        "description": "Test that the new capability is working as expected.\nConfirm the feature described in \"Allow evaluation of constant locals and variables prior to…\" is functioning correctly."
+      }
+    ],
+    "resolution": {
+      "summary": "Yes that would be the largest functional difference on the CLI side.  Defaults could still be used if a variable is no passed in via -var or tfvars.  If a default is not given and it is not specified it would be considered missing and result in an error.\n\nWe would handle it the same way we handle someone editing the TF files between init and plan.",

+    }
+  },

@@ -0,0 +1,73 @@
+{
+  "version": "kc-mission-v1",
+  "name": "opentofu-1042-allow-evaluation-of-constant-locals-and-variables-prior-to-module-",
+  "missionClass": "solution",
+  "author": "KubeStellar Bot",
+  "authorGithub": "kubestellar",
+  "mission": {
+    "title": "opentofu: Allow evaluation of constant locals and variables prior to module configuration",
+    "description": "Allow evaluation of constant locals and variables prior to module configuration. Requested by 150+ users.",
+    "type": "feature",
+    "status": "completed",
+    "steps": [
+      {
+        "title": "Check current opentofu deployment",
+        "description": "Verify your opentofu version and configuration:\n```bash\nkubectl get pods -n opentofu -l app.kubernetes.io/name=opentofu\nhelm list -n opentofu 2>/dev/null || echo \"Not installed via Helm\"\n```\nThis feature requires a working opentofu installation."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Review opentofu configuration",
+        "description": "Inspect the relevant opentofu configuration:\n```bash\nkubectl get all -n opentofu -l app.kubernetes.io/name=opentofu\nkubectl get configmap -n opentofu -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=opentofu\n```\nMany users expect to be able to DRY up their large configurations by using locals/variables in module sources and backend configurations (among other places)."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Apply the fix for Allow evaluation of constant locals and variables prior to",
+        "description": "This is the initial implementation of a static evaluation context that can be used to evaluate expression that do not rely on any state data (vars/locals only). \n\nThis pretty closely follows the plan detailed in RFC #1042 as I had already started experimenting with this code and knew roughly what I needed to change.  All of the examples detailed in the RFC should be functional with this PR.\n\nCurrent state of this work: Technical Preview.  Most components should be functional, but may have\n\nSee the fix PR for details: linked PR"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Verify the feature works",
+        "description": "Test that the new capability is working as expected:\n```bash\nkubectl get pods -n opentofu -l app.kubernetes.io/name=opentofu\nkubectl get events -n opentofu --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10\n```\nConfirm the feature described in \"Allow evaluation of constant locals and variables prior to\" is functioning correctly."
+      }
+    ],
+    "resolution": {
+      "summary": "This is the initial implementation of a static evaluation context that can be used to evaluate expression that do not rely on any state data (vars/locals only). \n\nThis pretty closely follows the plan detailed in RFC #1042 as I had already started experimenting with this code and knew roughly what I needed to change.  All of the examples detailed in the RFC should be functional with this PR.",
+      "codeSnippets": []
+    }
+  },
